title: Electroporation Using BioRad MicroPulser
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bk6v8","text":"Electroporation is a process in which brief electrical pulses create pores in a cell membrane, where DNA can enter. In this particular case, we are introducing plasmid DNA into E. coli using electroporation and a BioRad MicroPulser is used for DNA uptake by cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw one colony of E. coli cells from -80 °C freezer on ice
Mix 40-50 µL component cells and plasmid DNA in chilled Eppendorf tubes
Incubate cells for an hour at 37 degrees Celsius
Chill gene pulse cuvettes and Eppendorf tubes
Transfer mixture to the chilled cuvette, tap to sure there are no air bubbles
Add 0.5 mL SOC media immediately after to rescue cells
Transfer mixture to a 14 mL tube with a snap-cap
1 µL of DNA to a separate tube, flick to remove air bubbles, place on ice immediately
creator: Paulina Garcia
